title: Susan, A Kind Providence
content:
He dropt a tear on Susan's bier,
        He seem'd a most despairing swain;
    But bluer sky brought newer tie,
        And, would he wish her back again?

    The moments fly, and when we die,
        Will Philly Thistletop complain?
    She'll cry and sigh, and, dry her eye,
        And let herself be woo'd again.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Frederick Locker-Lampson, titled "Susan, A Kind Providence", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
